user.title: MXZ Coordinator
user.description: Select the two indoor heads on your MXZ outdoor unit and a temperature sensor for each room. The first head is the PRIMARY (it wins a heat-vs-cool standoff). Vane controls are detected automatically. Comfort thresholds, weather-based changeover, and fan boost are set afterward via the integration's Configure button.

data_description.notify_service: Who to notify when a head is knocked off its coordinated mode and the coordinator puts it back — e.g. notify.mobile_app_phone. Leave empty for no notifications.
data_description.primary_climate: This room WINS when the two rooms disagree (one wants heat, one wants cool).
data_description.secondary_climate: The second indoor unit on this outdoor unit.
data_description.primary_sensor: The thermometer the coordinator should trust for this room — a standalone sensor near where people actually sit beats the head's own reading (which runs warm when idle).
data_description.secondary_sensor: The thermometer the coordinator should trust for this room — a standalone sensor near where people actually sit beats the head's own reading (which runs warm when idle).

tuning.title: Comfort tuning (all optional)
tuning.description: Every knob, pre-filled with sensible defaults for your temperature unit — just hit Submit to accept them. You can change any of these later via the integration's Configure button.

data.demand_threshold: Demand threshold S (° off-target before the shared mode may flip)
data.engage_deadband: Re-engage drift (how far a coasting room may wander before conditioning resumes)
data.mode_hysteresis: Mode hysteresis (seconds minimum dwell before a heat↔cool flip)
data.resting_mode_bias: Resting mode when no room is calling
data.heat_lockout_floor: Heat-lockout safety floor (° — heat below this even when locked out)
data.cool_lockout_ceiling: Cool-lockout safety ceiling (° — cool above this even when locked out)
data.changeover_entity: Seasonal changeover weather/temperature entity (optional — auto-drives the lockouts from local weather)
data.fan_boost_enable: Fan boost: drive fan speed by how far the room is from target

data_description.demand_threshold: How far off target a room must be before the whole system may SWITCH between heating and cooling. Bigger = fewer mode changes. Most homes are fine with the default.
data_description.engage_deadband: After a room reaches its target it coasts with the fan. This is how far it may drift before conditioning kicks back in. Enter a value between 0.5–5 °F (0.25–2.5 °C).
data_description.mode_hysteresis: Minimum SECONDS between heating↔cooling switches — protects the compressor from rapid cycling. 600 = 10 minutes. Don't lower this unless you know why.
data_description.eco_cool_max: In away/eco mode the system stays off unless a room gets warmer than this. Think of it as vacation overheat protection.
data_description.eco_heat_min: In away/eco mode the system stays off unless a room gets colder than this. Vacation freeze protection.
data_description.clamp_min: The lowest setpoint your indoor units physically accept. Only change if your model's range differs — commands outside it get rejected by the unit.
data_description.clamp_max: The highest setpoint your indoor units physically accept.
data_description.resting_mode_bias: When no room needs anything, which mode should the system sit in? 'Last called' is right for most; pin cool or heat if your climate only ever needs one.
data_description.heat_lockout_floor: Even when heating is locked out for the season, a room colder than this still gets heat. A safety net, not a comfort setting.
data_description.cool_lockout_ceiling: Even when cooling is locked out for the season, a room hotter than this still gets cooling.
data_description.changeover_entity: Optional but recommended: pick your weather integration (or an outdoor temperature sensor) and the seasons handle themselves — heating locks out on warm days, cooling on cold ones. No weather entity? Add Home Assistant's built-in Met.no first.
data_description.changeover_heat_above: When the forecast daily high is at or above this, heating locks out (summer behavior).
data_description.changeover_cool_below: When the forecast daily high is at or below this, cooling locks out (winter behavior).
data_description.fan_boost_enable: Ramps the fan harder the further a room is from target, easing off as it arrives — like a car's auto climate. Recommended on.
data_description.fan_boost_max: The fastest fan speed the boost may use. Lower it if full blast is too loud for a bedroom.

error.same_head: The primary and secondary heads must be different entities.

abort.already_configured: This pair of heads is already configured.

abort.empty_options: No settings were submitted; the existing configuration was kept.

init.title: MXZ Coordinator tuning
init.description: All temperatures are in your Home Assistant unit (°C or °F); the defaults adapt to it.

data.demand_threshold: Demand threshold S (° off-target before the shared mode may flip)
data.engage_deadband: Re-engage drift (how far a coasting room may wander before conditioning resumes)
data.mode_hysteresis: Mode hysteresis (seconds minimum dwell before a heat↔cool flip)
data.resting_mode_bias: Resting mode when no room is calling
data.heat_lockout_floor: Heat-lockout safety floor (° — heat below this even when locked out)
data.cool_lockout_ceiling: Cool-lockout safety ceiling (° — cool above this even when locked out)
data.changeover_entity: Seasonal changeover weather/temperature entity (optional — auto-drives the lockouts from local weather)
data.fan_boost_enable: Fan boost: drive fan speed by how far the room is from target
data.primary_vane_vertical: Primary vertical vane (auto-detected; override if needed)
data.primary_vane_horizontal: Primary horizontal vane (auto-detected; override if needed)
data.secondary_vane_vertical: Secondary vertical vane (auto-detected; override if needed)
data.secondary_vane_horizontal: Secondary horizontal vane (auto-detected; override if needed)

data_description.primary_vane_vertical: Auto-detected at setup — only change if the detection picked the wrong select entity.
data_description.primary_vane_horizontal: Auto-detected at setup — only change if the detection picked the wrong select entity.
data_description.secondary_vane_vertical: Auto-detected at setup — only change if the detection picked the wrong select entity.
data_description.secondary_vane_horizontal: Auto-detected at setup — only change if the detection picked the wrong select entity.

recompute.name: Recompute
recompute.description: Force the coordinator to recompute the plan and re-apply it to the heads now.
